proof fn phi_1_get_proc_leaks_container_dom_without_wf(pm: ProcessManager, proc_ptr: ProcPtr)
    requires
        pm.proc_perms_wf(),
        pm.process_fields_wf(),
        pm.proc_dom().contains(proc_ptr),
        !pm.wf(),
    ensures
        pm.container_dom().contains(pm.get_proc(proc_ptr).owning_container),
{
}
reason: get_proc's external_body ensures `self.wf() ==> container_dom contains owning_container` but the ensures clause is guarded by wf() — yet the spec_get_proc inline spec has no such guarantee, creating a gap between the exec ensures and the spec recommends

name: get_thread_leaks_container_scheduler_wf

proof fn phi_2_get_thread_leaks_container_scheduler_wf(pm: ProcessManager, t_ptr: ThreadPtr)
    requires
        pm.wf(),
        pm.thread_dom().contains(t_ptr),
    ensures
        pm.get_container(pm.get_thread(t_ptr).owning_container).scheduler.wf(),
        pm.get_container(pm.get_thread(t_ptr).owning_container).owned_procs.wf(),
        pm.get_container(pm.get_thread(t_ptr).owning_container).children.wf(),
{
}
reason: get_thread's external_body ensures wf() for scheduler/owned_procs/children of the owning container — these should follow from wf() invariants, but being in external_body means they're trusted without proof and could mask missing invariant clauses

name: owned_threads_shared_across_containers
code:
proof fn phi_3_owned_threads_shared_across_containers(
    pm: ProcessManager,
    c1: ContainerPtr,
    c2: ContainerPtr,
    t: ThreadPtr,
)
    requires
        pm.wf(),
        pm.container_dom().contains(c1),
        pm.container_dom().contains(c2),
        c1 != c2,
        pm.get_container(c1).owned_threads@.contains(t),
        pm.get_container(c2).owned_threads@.contains(t),
    ensures
        true,
{
}
reason: No disjointness constraint on owned_threads across containers — two containers can claim the same thread simultaneously despite the resource ownership model requiring exclusive ownership

name: inactive_cpu_has_arbitrary_owning_container
code:
proof fn phi_5_inactive_cpu_has_arbitrary_owning_container(pm: ProcessManager, cpu_i: CpuId)
    requires
        pm.wf(),
        0 <= cpu_i < NUM_CPUS,
        pm.cpu_list@[cpu_i as int].active == false,
    ensures
        pm.container_dom().contains(pm.cpu_list@[cpu_i as int].owning_container),
{
}
reason: container_cpu_wf requires ALL cpus (active or not) to have owning_container in container_dom — inactive CPUs should arguably have no meaningful owning_container, yet the spec forces a valid container binding even when the CPU is not running
